data.finding: GESP C++/Python level 5 syllabus lists elementary number theory, linked lists, binary search/answer, recursion, divide-and-conquer, greedy, and complexity; it does not list dynamic programming.
data.significance: Level+algorithm query must distinguish official syllabus fit from inferred tags.

data.finding: Simple dynamic programming appears in level 6; complex dynamic programming appears in level 7.
data.significance: A query for GESP level 5 + DP should produce an out-of-level or boundary warning.

data.finding: Official GESP site publishes true-question entries by exam date, with per-language/per-level PDF links.
data.significance: This should be the primary crawler seed source.

data.finding: A third-party page lists GESP level 5 problem IDs and titles from 2023-2025, useful for OJ mapping and validation.
data.significance: Useful secondary source, but labels and solutions are non-official.

data.description: Full problem statement republication may violate source copyright or platform terms.
data.mitigation: Store metadata, short evidence snippets, and source links by default; only store full text where license or permission allows.

data.recommendation: Make source_document and tag_evidence mandatory for every problem tag.
